songs.title: Qu-Qu-Quack
songs.lyrics:
[Intro — whispered]
kw… kw… kw…
(two sounds, one quick glide)

[Hook 1 — kids chant]
Qu! Qu! Kw-kw-kw!
What begins with Qu?
Qu-qu-queen! Qu-qu-queen!
It's a queen! (a queen!)

[Hook 2 — kids chant]
Qu! Qu! Kw-kw-kw!
What begins with Qu?
Qu-qu-quilt! Qu-qu-quilt!
It's a quilt! (a quilt!)

[Hook 3 — kids chant]
Qu! Qu! Kw-kw-kw!
What begins with Qu?
Qu-qu-quill! Qu-qu-quill!
It's a quill! (a quill!)

[Breakdown — whispered, bass drops out]
kw… kw… (q brings u everywhere it goes)
Q-U… /k/ and /w/… kw… kw…
quack… quick… quiz… (qu starts them all!)

[Bridge]
A potato?! (NO!)
P-p-potato! Not Qu!
(kw… kw… kw…)

[Final Hook — big]
Qu! Qu! Kw-kw-kw!
Qu-qu-quack! Qu-qu-quack!
The duck says quack, quack, quack!
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: Qu is a BLEND of two sounds /k/+/w/ = 'kw', glided quick and round, NEVER 'cue' (letter name). Teach the rule inside the song: q never travels alone — it always brings u with it (qu). Land the final hook on the duck's quack, the anchor word. Keep 'kw' crisp, not 'kwuh'.

songs.title: The Duck Says Quack
songs.lyrics:
[Intro]
(quack? quack?)

[Hook]
The duck says, "Quack!"
The duck says, "Quack!"
What does the duck say? (quack! quack!)
The duck says, "Quack!"

[Verse 2]
The cat says, "Quack!"?
The cat says, "Quack!"?
(No! That is not right!)
But the cat says, "Quack!"

[Verse 3]
The dog says, "Quack!"
The fox says, "Quack!"
Quick, quick! Everyone!
(Quack! Quack! Quack!)

[Verse 4]
Sam says, "Quack!"
Segina says, "Quack!"
What does the duck say? (QUACK!)
Quit it! says the duck!

[Outro]
The potato says…?
(POTATO! QUACK!)
The duck says, "Quack."
(quack… shhh…)
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: Introduces the glue word 'says' — a heart word (it does NOT read the way it looks; teach it whole, never blend it). One frame: 'The ___ says, "Quack!"' The comedy is the WHOLE cast quacking wrong until the duck says 'Quit it!'. Kids choir answers (quack! quack!). Every qu word (quack, quick, quit) is the 'kw' blend.

spreads.artNote: The Duck (white) center stage, chest puffed, beak open, clearly about to teach everyone something. A confident quack coming.
The Duck mid-quack, beak wide, a little 'QUACK!' speech puff. The heart word 'says' printed for the first time — point to it.
The Cat (tabby) attempting to quack, mouth in a ridiculous duck-shape, deeply unconvinced of itself. Comedy gold.
The Dog (brown) quacking with total commitment, head thrown back, thrilled to join in even though it's absurd.
The Rat (grey) doing tiny high-pitched double quacks, on tiptoe, giving it everything.
Sam the peg-doll boy cupping his hands to his mouth, quacking, cheeks round, joyful.
The whole cast in a quacking chorus — cat, dog, rat, fox, Sam, Segina all mid-quack, the Duck conducting like a bandleader. Peak chaos.
The Fox (red) sly-quacking out the side of its mouth, one eyebrow up, not really trying. Cast callback.
The potato, spotlit, a tiny 'QUACK!' puff coming from it — the potato has joined the chorus. No noun printed — the class SHOUTS the word (and the quack!) from the picture.

extras: The q+u rule: q NEVER travels alone — pin sandpaper q and u together as one tile, they go everywhere as 'qu' (kw)
Heart-word 'says': teach it whole on a gold card — it does not read the way it looks, so we know it by sight, never sound it out
Animal-sound circle: everyone tries to say 'quack' like the duck, then each real animal's real sound — the duck says 'quit it!'

REUSED FROM WEEK 1 — a potato wearing tiny round glasses standing at a miniature whiteboard with a pointer, teaching, deadpan, ultra-realistic photograph, single subject centered, dramatic spotlight on deep forest-green backdrop, soft shadows, cinematic, slightly whimsical --ar 3:2 --style raw

teacherFocus: Qu is a blend of two sounds — /k/+/w/ = 'kw' — and it carries the year's spelling rule: q never travels alone, it always brings u. Pin sandpaper q+u as one tile. Introduce the heart word 'says' whole on a gold card — it does not read the way it looks, so we know it by sight and never sound it out. The whole cast quacking wrong is the joke; let them try every animal sound.
